title: CI/CD Pipelines: Design, Deliver, Govern
summary: CI/CD as a design discipline — pipeline topology, branching strategies, security gates, supply-chain attestations (SLSA, Sigstore, SBOM), DORA metrics, and the patterns that ship software safely at scale.

volatileChapters.reason: GitHub Actions / GitLab Pipelines / CircleCI feature parity shifts quarterly; managed-runner pricing and vendor lock-in vectors evolve faster than the rest of the book.
AI-augmented pipelines (LLM-generated workflow diffs, MCP-based agentic deployment) and agent-era CI patterns are the fastest-moving frontier in delivery tooling.

inspiredBy.title: Continuous Integration (CI) and Continuous Delivery (CD): A Practical Guide to Designing and Developing Pipelines

chapters.title: Why Pipelines Are Design, Not Tooling
chapters.summary: Most teams' pipelines are accidental architectures glued together from defaults — but a pipeline is a system with four jobs (build, verify, secure, deploy), and treating it as a design problem is the difference between shipping safely and shipping luckily.

chapters.title: Branching Strategies and Their CI Cost
chapters.summary: Branch model dictates pipeline complexity — trunk-based dev makes high-frequency CI cheap, GitFlow makes it expensive, and merge queues exist to fix the race conditions trunk-based development creates at scale.

chapters.title: Pipeline Topology
chapters.summary: Linear vs fan-out/fan-in, matrix builds, reusable workflows, monorepo affected-builds, and pipeline-of-pipelines — the structural choices that determine whether CI scales with the team or chokes it.

chapters.title: Source Control Hygiene as Pipeline Foundation
chapters.summary: Conventional commits, signed commits, codeowners, protected branches, and commit-graph hygiene — the version-control disciplines that make `git bisect` work and pipelines deterministic.

chapters.title: Build Stage Design
chapters.summary: Compile vs package vs containerize, build determinism, hermetic builds, remote build caches, and SBOM generation as a build-time artifact — turning the build stage from a black box into an auditable, reproducible boundary.

chapters.title: Test Stage Design
chapters.summary: The test pyramid in CI terms — unit, integration, contract, E2E — flaky-test management, test sharding for parallel execution, and selective testing for monorepos that gets feedback to the developer in minutes, not hours.

chapters.title: Security Gates in the Pipeline
chapters.summary: DevSecOps as design, not bolted-on stage — SAST/DAST/SCA placement, secret scanning, dependency pinning, Sigstore signing, and SLSA L2/L3 build attestations that make the pipeline itself the security boundary.

chapters.title: Pipeline as Code
chapters.summary: YAML/HCL DSLs, reusable workflow libraries, pipeline modules, testing your pipeline definitions, and the recursion problem of pipelines that build pipelines.

chapters.title: Deployment Strategies
chapters.summary: Recreate, Rolling, Blue/Green, Canary, Shadow, Ring, Dark Launch — when each is correct, how auto-rollback closes the loop, and the bake-time discipline that turns deployment into a non-event.

chapters.title: Progressive Delivery and Feature Flags
chapters.summary: Decoupling deploy from release — flag systems (LaunchDarkly, OpenFeature), flag debt and lifecycle, A/B testing infrastructure, and the experimentation pipeline that turns CI/CD into a learning loop.

chapters.title: Measuring Delivery Performance
chapters.summary: DORA's four metrics — deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, MTTR — measured from real pipeline metadata, and the SPACE framework as DORA's qualitative companion.

chapters.title: Tools Landscape
chapters.summary: GitHub Actions vs GitLab Pipelines vs CircleCI vs Buildkite vs Jenkins — managed runners vs self-hosted, cost models, vendor lock-in vectors, and the calculus of when to switch tools vs when to invest in the one you have.

chapters.title: CI/CD in 2026+
chapters.summary: AI-augmented pipelines — LLM-generated workflow diffs, autoremediation, MCP-based agentic deployment, AI-era build-cache patterns — and the framing for distinguishing what's load-bearing in the agent era from what's hype.
